overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from Batman: Arkham Knight showing the character traversing a rainy Gotham City rooftop at night. The player overlooks neon-lit streets and intersections from elevated vantage points before ascending an exterior stairway to a higher level.

events.description: Batman begins walking forward from initial stationary position toward rooftop edge
reaches the rooftop perimeter and begins looking down at the street below

agents.ego_description: Batman walks steadily across the rooftop toward the edge, cape flowing in the rain, surveying the city below

entered.detail: tall vertical neon sign with 'LACEY'S' lettering on the building facade
raised railway structure spanning across the city street with metal supports
large illuminated advertisement screen showing colorful graphics
tall building with ornate architectural details and lit windows
wet asphalt street with moving vehicle headlights visible through the rain

segments.scene_description: The segment opens on a rain-soaked Gotham City rooftop at night. Batman walks across the concrete surface toward the edge, with the prominent LACEY'S department store sign visible to the left. The camera follows as he approaches the perimeter, revealing the elevated train tracks and digital billboards that characterize the dense urban downtown environment below.

scene_state.functional_zone: narrow rooftop ledge with safety railings

events.description: Batman turns right to follow the narrow ledge along the building wall
approaches the exterior stairway entrance at the corner of the building

agents.ego_description: Batman carefully walks along the narrow rooftop ledge, maintaining balance while looking down at the street traffic below

entered.detail: tall stone and brick facade with weathered texture and small windows
rusted metal railings along the rooftop edge for fall protection
opening leading to exterior concrete stairs ascending to upper level

segments.scene_description: Moving along the narrow perimeter of the rooftop, Batman hugs the brick building wall on his right while the metal safety railing lines the left edge. The LACEY'S sign and main billboard fade from view as he progresses toward a corner stairway entrance, with the wet city streets still visible far below through the driving rain.

events.description: Batman enters the enclosed exterior stairway from the open rooftop
continues ascending the stairs toward the upper rooftop platform

agents.ego_description: Batman climbs the concrete stairs between building walls, moving from the lower rooftop to an elevated position

entered.detail: weathered concrete steps with puddles and rain runoff
rusted metal pipes serving as handrails along the stairway
large ventilation pipes and ductwork attached to building wall
elevated flat roof area visible at the top of the stairs

segments.scene_description: Batman transitions from the open rooftop into a more confined exterior stairway sandwiched between gothic building walls. The environment becomes sheltered from the direct rain as he ascends the concrete steps, passing industrial piping and ventilation equipment. The camera follows his climb toward the upper rooftop platform, leaving the street view behind.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement across a rain-soaked rooftop that gradually transitions into a confined architectural ascent. It begins with Batman walking across a concrete surface toward the roof's edge, passing a prominent LACEY'S department store sign while the dense urban downtown and elevated train tracks unfold below. As he moves along the narrow perimeter, the camera hugs a brick wall and follows him past a metal safety railing, causing the main billboards to fade from view amidst the driving rain. The scene then shifts from the open rooftop into a sheltered exterior stairway sandwiched between gothic walls, where the camera tracks his climb past industrial piping and ventilation equipment. Finally, the perspective ascends the concrete steps toward an upper platform, leaving the distant street view behind.
